## Variables

<a name="SupportedExts"></a>SupportedExts are universally supported extensions.

```go
var SupportedExts = []string{"json", "toml", "yaml", "yml", "properties", "props", "prop", "hcl", "tfvars", "dotenv", "env", "ini"}
```

<a name="SupportedRemoteProviders"></a>SupportedRemoteProviders are universally supported remote providers.

```go
var SupportedRemoteProviders = []string{"etcd", "etcd3", "consul", "firestore", "nats"}
```

<a name="AddConfigPath"></a>

## func [AddConfigPath](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L434>)

```go
func AddConfigPath(in string)
```

AddConfigPath adds a path for Viper to search for the config file in. Can be called multiple times to define multiple search paths.

<a name="AddRemoteProvider"></a>

## func [AddRemoteProvider](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L93>)

```go
func AddRemoteProvider(provider, endpoint, path string) error
```

AddRemoteProvider adds a remote configuration source. Remote Providers are searched in the order they are added. provider is a string value: "etcd", "etcd3", "consul", "firestore" or "nats" are currently supported. endpoint is the url. etcd requires http://ip:port, consul requires ip:port, nats requires nats://ip:port path is the path in the k/v store to retrieve configuration To retrieve a config file called myapp.json from /configs/myapp.json you should set path to /configs and set config name \(SetConfigName\(\)\) to "myapp".

<a name="AddSecureRemoteProvider"></a>

## func [AddSecureRemoteProvider](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L134>)

```go
func AddSecureRemoteProvider(provider, endpoint, path, secretkeyring string) error
```

AddSecureRemoteProvider adds a remote configuration source. Secure Remote Providers are searched in the order they are added. provider is a string value: "etcd", "etcd3", "consul", "firestore" or "nats" are currently supported. endpoint is the url. etcd requires http://ip:port consul requires ip:port secretkeyring is the filepath to your openpgp secret keyring. e.g. /etc/secrets/myring.gpg path is the path in the k/v store to retrieve configuration To retrieve a config file called myapp.json from /configs/myapp.json you should set path to /configs and set config name \(SetConfigName\(\)\) to "myapp". Secure Remote Providers are implemented with github.com/sagikazarmark/crypt.

<a name="AllKeys"></a>

## func [AllKeys](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1966>)

```go
func AllKeys() []string
```

AllKeys returns all keys holding a value, regardless of where they are set. Nested keys are returned with a v.keyDelim separator.

<a name="AllSettings"></a>

## func [AllSettings](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L2050>)

```go
func AllSettings() map[string]any
```

AllSettings merges all settings and returns them as a map\[string\]any.

<a name="AllowEmptyEnv"></a>

## func [AllowEmptyEnv](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L400>)

```go
func AllowEmptyEnv(allowEmptyEnv bool)
```

AllowEmptyEnv tells Viper to consider set, but empty environment variables as valid values instead of falling back. For backward compatibility reasons this is false by default.

<a name="AutomaticEnv"></a>

## func [AutomaticEnv](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1446>)

```go
func AutomaticEnv()
```

AutomaticEnv makes Viper check if environment variables match any of the existing keys \(config, default or flags\). If matching env vars are found, they are loaded into Viper.

<a name="BindEnv"></a>

## func [BindEnv](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1147>)

```go
func BindEnv(input ...string) error
```

BindEnv binds a Viper key to a ENV variable. ENV variables are case sensitive. If only a key is provided, it will use the env key matching the key, uppercased. If more arguments are provided, they will represent the env variable names that should bind to this key and will be taken in the specified order. EnvPrefix will be used when set when env name is not provided.

<a name="BindFlagValue"></a>

## func [BindFlagValue](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1130>)

```go
func BindFlagValue(key string, flag FlagValue) error
```

BindFlagValue binds a specific key to a FlagValue.

<a name="BindFlagValues"></a>

## func [BindFlagValues](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1116>)

```go
func BindFlagValues(flags FlagValueSet) error
```

BindFlagValues binds a full FlagValue set to the configuration, using each flag's long name as the config key.

<a name="BindPFlag"></a>

## func [BindPFlag](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1100>)

```go
func BindPFlag(key string, flag *pflag.Flag) error
```

BindPFlag binds a specific key to a pflag \(as used by cobra\). Example \(where serverCmd is a Cobra instance\):

```
serverCmd.Flags().Int("port", 1138, "Port to run Application server on")
Viper.BindPFlag("port", serverCmd.Flags().Lookup("port"))
```

<a name="BindPFlags"></a>

## func [BindPFlags](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1087>)

```go
func BindPFlags(flags *pflag.FlagSet) error
```

BindPFlags binds a full flag set to the configuration, using each flag's long name as the config key.

<a name="DebugTo"></a>

```go
func DebugTo(w io.Writer)
```

<a name="Get"></a>

## func [Get](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L706>)

```go
func Get(key string) any
```

Get can retrieve any value given the key to use. Get is case\-insensitive for a key. Get has the behavior of returning the value associated with the first place from where it is set. Viper will check in the following order: override, flag, env, config file, key/value store, default

Get returns an interface. For a specific value use one of the Get\_\_\_\_ methods.

<a name="SetOptions"></a>

## func [SetOptions](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L251>)

```go
func SetOptions(opts ...Option)
```

SetOptions sets the options on the global Viper instance.

Be careful when using this function: subsequent calls may override options you set. It's always better to use a local Viper instance.

<a name="SetTypeByDefaultValue"></a>

## func [SetTypeByDefaultValue](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L674>)

```go
func SetTypeByDefaultValue(enable bool)
```

SetTypeByDefaultValue enables or disables the inference of a key value's type when the Get function is used based upon a key's default value as opposed to the value returned based on the normal fetch logic.

For example, if a key has a default value of \[\]string\{\} and the same key is set via an environment variable to "a b c", a call to the Get function would return a string slice for the key if the key's type is inferred by the default value and the Get function would return:

```
[]string {"a", "b", "c"}
```

Otherwise the Get function would return:

```
"a b c"
```

<a name="Finder"></a>

## type [Finder](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/finder.go#L21-L23>)

Finder looks for files and directories in an \[afero.Fs\] filesystem.

```go
type Finder interface {
    Find(fsys afero.Fs) ([]string, error)
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"

 "github.com/sagikazarmark/locafero"
 "github.com/spf13/afero"

 "github.com/spf13/viper"
)

func main() {
 fs := afero.NewMemMapFs()

 fs.Mkdir("/home/user", 0o777)

 f, _ := fs.Create("/home/user/myapp.yaml")
 f.WriteString("foo: bar")
 f.Close()

 // HCL will have a "lower" priority in the search order
 fs.Create("/home/user/myapp.hcl")

 finder := locafero.Finder{
  Paths: []string{"/home/user"},
  Names: locafero.NameWithExtensions("myapp", viper.SupportedExts...),
  Type:  locafero.FileTypeFile, // This is important!
 }

 v := viper.NewWithOptions(viper.WithFinder(finder))
 v.SetFs(fs)
 v.ReadInConfig()

 fmt.Println(v.GetString("foo"))

}
```

#### Output

```
bar
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Finders"></a>

### func [Finders](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/finder.go#L26>)

```go
func Finders(finders ...Finder) Finder
```

Finders combines multiple finders into one.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"

 "github.com/sagikazarmark/locafero"
 "github.com/spf13/afero"

 "github.com/spf13/viper"
)

func main() {
 fs := afero.NewMemMapFs()

 fs.Mkdir("/home/user", 0o777)
 f, _ := fs.Create("/home/user/myapp.yaml")
 f.WriteString("foo: bar")
 f.Close()

 fs.Mkdir("/etc/myapp", 0o777)
 fs.Create("/etc/myapp/config.yaml")

 // Combine multiple finders to search for files in multiple locations with different criteria
 finder := viper.Finders(
  locafero.Finder{
   Paths: []string{"/home/user"},
   Names: locafero.NameWithExtensions("myapp", viper.SupportedExts...),
   Type:  locafero.FileTypeFile, // This is important!
  },
  locafero.Finder{
   Paths: []string{"/etc/myapp"},
   Names: []string{"config.yaml"}, // Only accept YAML files in the system config directory
   Type:  locafero.FileTypeFile,   // This is important!
  },
 )

 v := viper.NewWithOptions(viper.WithFinder(finder))
 v.SetFs(fs)
 v.ReadInConfig()

 fmt.Println(v.GetString("foo"))

}
```

#### Output

```
bar
```

</p>
</details>

## type [Option](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L190-L192>)

Option configures Viper using the functional options paradigm popularized by Rob Pike and Dave Cheney. If you're unfamiliar with this style, see https://commandcenter.blogspot.com/2014/01/self-referential-functions-and-design.html and https://dave.cheney.net/2014/10/17/functional-options-for-friendly-apis.

```go
type Option interface {
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="RemoteConfigError"></a>

## type [RemoteConfigError](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L44>)

RemoteConfigError denotes encountering an error while trying to pull the configuration from the remote provider.

```go
type RemoteConfigError string
```

<a name="RemoteConfigError.Error"></a>

### func \(RemoteConfigError\) [Error](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L47>)

```go
func (rce RemoteConfigError) Error() string
```

Error returns the formatted remote provider error.

<a name="RemoteProvider"></a>

## type [RemoteProvider](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L78-L83>)

RemoteProvider stores the configuration necessary to connect to a remote key/value store. Optional secretKeyring to unencrypt encrypted values can be provided.

```go
type RemoteProvider interface {
    Provider() string
    Endpoint() string
    Path() string
    SecretKeyring() string
}
```

<a name="RemoteResponse"></a>

## type [RemoteResponse](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L25-L28>)

RemoteResponse represents a response from a remote configuration provider.

```go
type RemoteResponse struct {
    Value []byte
    Error error
}
```

<a name="StringReplacer"></a>

## type [StringReplacer](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L209-L212>)

StringReplacer applies a set of replacements to a string.

```go
type StringReplacer interface {
    // Replace returns a copy of s with all replacements performed.
    Replace(s string) string
}
```

<a name="UnsupportedConfigError"></a>

## type [UnsupportedConfigError](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/errors.go#L88>)

UnsupportedConfigError denotes encountering an unsupported configuration filetype.

```go
type UnsupportedConfigError string
```

```go
func (str UnsupportedConfigError) Error() string
```

Error returns the formatted configuration error.

<a name="UnsupportedRemoteProviderError"></a>

## type [UnsupportedRemoteProviderError](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L35>)

UnsupportedRemoteProviderError denotes encountering an unsupported remote provider. Currently only etcd and Consul are supported.

```go
type UnsupportedRemoteProviderError string
```

<a name="UnsupportedRemoteProviderError.Error"></a>

### func \(UnsupportedRemoteProviderError\) [Error](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L38>)

```go
func (str UnsupportedRemoteProviderError) Error() string
```

Error returns the formatted remote provider error.

<a name="Viper"></a>

## type [Viper](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L107-L155>)

Viper is a prioritized configuration registry. It maintains a set of configuration sources, fetches values to populate those, and provides them according to the source's priority. The priority of the sources is the following: 1. overrides 2. flags 3. env. variables 4. config file 5. key/value store 6. defaults

For example, if values from the following sources were loaded:

```
Defaults : {
 "secret": "",
 "user": "default",
 "endpoint": "https://localhost"
}
Config : {
 "user": "root"
 "secret": "defaultsecret"
}
Env : {
 "secret": "somesecretkey"
}
```

The resulting config will have the following values:

```
{
 "secret": "somesecretkey",
 "user": "root",
 "endpoint": "https://localhost"
}
```

Note: Vipers are not safe for concurrent Get\(\) and Set\(\) operations.

```go
type Viper struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="GetViper"></a>

### func [GetViper](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L695>)

```go
func GetViper() *Viper
```

GetViper gets the global Viper instance.

<a name="New"></a>

### func [New](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L158>)

```go
func New() *Viper
```

New returns an initialized Viper instance.

<a name="NewWithOptions"></a>

### func [NewWithOptions](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L237>)

```go
func NewWithOptions(opts ...Option) *Viper
```

NewWithOptions creates a new Viper instance.

<a name="Sub"></a>

### func [Sub](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L766>)

```go
func Sub(key string) *Viper
```

Sub returns new Viper instance representing a sub tree of this instance. Sub is case\-insensitive for a key.

<a name="Viper.AddConfigPath"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [AddConfigPath](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L438>)

```go
func (v *Viper) AddConfigPath(in string)
```

AddConfigPath adds a path for Viper to search for the config file in. Can be called multiple times to define multiple search paths.

<a name="Viper.AddRemoteProvider"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [AddRemoteProvider](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L105>)

```go
func (v *Viper) AddRemoteProvider(provider, endpoint, path string) error
```

AddRemoteProvider adds a remote configuration source. Remote Providers are searched in the order they are added. provider is a string value: "etcd", "etcd3", "consul", "firestore" or "nats" are currently supported. endpoint is the url. etcd requires http://ip:port, consul requires ip:port, nats requires nats://ip:port path is the path in the k/v store to retrieve configuration To retrieve a config file called myapp.json from /configs/myapp.json you should set path to /configs and set config name \(SetConfigName\(\)\) to "myapp".

<a name="Viper.AddSecureRemoteProvider"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [AddSecureRemoteProvider](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/remote.go#L148>)

```go
func (v *Viper) AddSecureRemoteProvider(provider, endpoint, path, secretkeyring string) error
```

AddSecureRemoteProvider adds a remote configuration source. Secure Remote Providers are searched in the order they are added. provider is a string value: "etcd", "etcd3", "consul", "firestore" or "nats" are currently supported. endpoint is the url. etcd requires http://ip:port consul requires ip:port secretkeyring is the filepath to your openpgp secret keyring. e.g. /etc/secrets/myring.gpg path is the path in the k/v store to retrieve configuration To retrieve a config file called myapp.json from /configs/myapp.json you should set path to /configs and set config name \(SetConfigName\(\)\) to "myapp". Secure Remote Providers are implemented with github.com/sagikazarmark/crypt.

<a name="Viper.AllKeys"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [AllKeys](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1970>)

```go
func (v *Viper) AllKeys() []string
```

AllKeys returns all keys holding a value, regardless of where they are set. Nested keys are returned with a v.keyDelim separator.

<a name="Viper.AllSettings"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [AllSettings](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L2053>)

```go
func (v *Viper) AllSettings() map[string]any
```

AllSettings merges all settings and returns them as a map\[string\]any.

<a name="Viper.AllowEmptyEnv"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [AllowEmptyEnv](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L405>)

```go
func (v *Viper) AllowEmptyEnv(allowEmptyEnv bool)
```

AllowEmptyEnv tells Viper to consider set, but empty environment variables as valid values instead of falling back. For backward compatibility reasons this is false by default.

<a name="Viper.AutomaticEnv"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [AutomaticEnv](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1450>)

```go
func (v *Viper) AutomaticEnv()
```

AutomaticEnv makes Viper check if environment variables match any of the existing keys \(config, default or flags\). If matching env vars are found, they are loaded into Viper.

<a name="Viper.BindEnv"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [BindEnv](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1155>)

```go
func (v *Viper) BindEnv(input ...string) error
```

BindEnv binds a Viper key to a ENV variable. ENV variables are case sensitive. If only a key is provided, it will use the env key matching the key, uppercased. If more arguments are provided, they will represent the env variable names that should bind to this key and will be taken in the specified order. EnvPrefix will be used when set when env name is not provided.

<a name="Viper.BindFlagValue"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [BindFlagValue](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1133>)

```go
func (v *Viper) BindFlagValue(key string, flag FlagValue) error
```

BindFlagValue binds a specific key to a FlagValue.

<a name="Viper.BindFlagValues"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [BindFlagValues](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1120>)

```go
func (v *Viper) BindFlagValues(flags FlagValueSet) (err error)
```

BindFlagValues binds a full FlagValue set to the configuration, using each flag's long name as the config key.

<a name="Viper.BindPFlag"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [BindPFlag](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1107>)

```go
func (v *Viper) BindPFlag(key string, flag *pflag.Flag) error
```

BindPFlag binds a specific key to a pflag \(as used by cobra\). Example \(where serverCmd is a Cobra instance\):

```
serverCmd.Flags().Int("port", 1138, "Port to run Application server on")
Viper.BindPFlag("port", serverCmd.Flags().Lookup("port"))
```

<a name="Viper.BindPFlags"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [BindPFlags](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1091>)

```go
func (v *Viper) BindPFlags(flags *pflag.FlagSet) error
```

BindPFlags binds a full flag set to the configuration, using each flag's long name as the config key.

<a name="Viper.ConfigFileUsed"></a>

```go
func (v *Viper) ConfigFileUsed() string
```

ConfigFileUsed returns the file used to populate the config registry.

<a name="Viper.Get"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [Get](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L715>)

```go
func (v *Viper) Get(key string) any
```

Get retrieves the value associated with the key. Get is case\-insensitive for a key. Get has the behavior of returning the value associated with the first place from where it is set. Viper will check in the following order: override, flag, env, config file, key/value store, default

Get returns an interface. For a specific value use one of the Get\_\_\_\_ methods.

```go
func (v *Viper) ReadInConfig() error
```

ReadInConfig will discover and load the configuration file from disk and key/value stores, searching in one of the defined paths.

```go

```

ReadRemoteConfig attempts to get configuration from a remote source and read it in the remote configuration registry.

```go

```

RegisterAlias creates an alias that provides another accessor for the same key. This enables one to change a name without breaking the application.

```go
func (v *Viper) SafeWriteConfig() error
```

SafeWriteConfig writes current configuration to file only if the file does not exist.

```go

```

```go
func (v *Viper) Set(key string, value any)
```

Set sets the value for the key in the override register. Set is case\-insensitive for a key. Will be used instead of values obtained via flags, config file, ENV, default, or key/value store.

<a name="Viper.SetConfigFile"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetConfigFile](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L361>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetConfigFile(in string)
```

SetConfigFile explicitly defines the path, name and extension of the config file. Viper will use this and not check any of the config paths.

<a name="Viper.SetConfigName"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetConfigName](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L2090>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetConfigName(in string)
```

SetConfigName sets the name for the config file. Does not include extension.

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetConfigPermissions](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L2117>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetConfigPermissions(perm os.FileMode)
```

SetConfigPermissions sets the permissions for the config file.

<a name="Viper.SetConfigType"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetConfigType](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L2107>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetConfigType(in string)
```

SetConfigType sets the type of the configuration returned by the remote source, e.g. "json".

<a name="Viper.SetDefault"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetDefault](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1540>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetDefault(key string, value any)
```

SetDefault sets the default value for this key. SetDefault is case\-insensitive for a key. Default only used when no value is provided by the user via flag, config or ENV.

<a name="Viper.SetEnvKeyReplacer"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetEnvKeyReplacer](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L1462>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetEnvKeyReplacer(r *strings.Replacer)
```

SetEnvKeyReplacer sets the strings.Replacer on the viper object Useful for mapping an environmental variable to a key that does not match it.

<a name="Viper.SetEnvPrefix"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetEnvPrefix](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L375>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetEnvPrefix(in string)
```

SetEnvPrefix defines a prefix that ENVIRONMENT variables will use. E.g. if your prefix is "spf", the env registry will look for env variables that start with "SPF\_".

<a name="Viper.SetFs"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetFs](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L2080>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetFs(fs afero.Fs)
```

SetFs sets the filesystem to use to read configuration.

<a name="Viper.SetTypeByDefaultValue"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [SetTypeByDefaultValue](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L690>)

```go
func (v *Viper) SetTypeByDefaultValue(enable bool)
```

SetTypeByDefaultValue enables or disables the inference of a key value's type when the Get function is used based upon a key's default value as opposed to the value returned based on the normal fetch logic.

For example, if a key has a default value of \[\]string\{\} and the same key is set via an environment variable to "a b c", a call to the Get function would return a string slice for the key if the key's type is inferred by the default value and the Get function would return:

```
[]string {"a", "b", "c"}
```

Otherwise the Get function would return:

```
"a b c"
```

<a name="Viper.Sub"></a>

### func \(\*Viper\) [Sub](<https://github.com/spf13/viper/blob/master/viper.go#L770>)

```go
func (v *Viper) Sub(key string) *Viper
```

Sub returns a new Viper instance representing a sub tree of this instance. Sub is case\-insensitive for a key.

Unmarshal unmarshals the config into a Struct. Make sure that the tags on the fields of the structure are properly set.
